Design Starting
Click to view screens

Slot Allocation

Staff can view slot offers ranked by age-graded performance, accept or decline on behalf of athletes, and process registration with tap-to-pay. The full athlete list shows division rank, original time, age-graded time, and current status.

  • Current slot offer card with accept / decline actions
  • Complete athlete list with division rank and age-graded time
  • Status tracking: Waiting, Accepted, Passed, Failed
  • Athlete information pre-filled from previous registration
  • Secure payment redirect flow
ⓘ  Feature complete — pending approval for App Store and Play Store release.

Edit Race

Coordinators can edit race configuration directly from the app. Key fields that risk state desync are now read-only; remaining fields remain fully editable.

  • Editable: Location, qualifier settings, documents
  • Read-only: Race name, date, status, course type, wave start times

Broadcasting 1.0

Staff can create and publish notifications from the web or the app. Messages are sent as push notifications or in-chat messages and received directly on the app by targeted audience groups such as Medical Workers, IRONMAN Staff, and Volunteers.

  • Notification type: Push or In-Chat
  • Title, message body, and optional destination link
  • Optional media attachment (up to 10MB)
  • Audience targeting by role group
  • iOS and Android preview before publishing
  • Estimated recipient count with opt-out awareness

Global Dashboard

Multi-race operational overview for ROC supervisors — one interface covering all ongoing events, live incident feeds, athlete counts, and a global race map.

  • Single view of all active races — no more switching between systems during race day
  • Live incident feed with dispatch shortcuts, race clock, and weather per event
  • Global race map and directory covering all upcoming and finished events
ⓘ  Ready for development start.

Incident Management 1.0

End-to-end incident lifecycle from field report to command-center resolution, with live course map and full athlete medical context.

  • First version prioritized by incident type: Medical, Weather, and Transportation
  • Field staff report on mobile, coordinators manage on the web command center with a live course map
  • Full athlete medical context at every step, from first report to resolution
  • Real-time resource tracking: medics, ambulances, helicopters, vehicles per race
ⓘ  Ready for development start.

PCR

Replaces paper-based medical records on race day — full documentation from QR scan to discharge, tied to the athlete profile.

  • QR scan links directly to athlete medical profile at check-in
  • Field staff complete structured PCR forms on mobile, end-to-end from incident to discharge
  • All records tied to the athlete profile and accessible to the command center
  • Awaiting Medical team validation before scope is locked
ⓘ  Awaiting inputs from the Medical team.

Live Activities

A real-time operational event log for the ROC — a chronological feed of everything significant that happens during a race. Think of it as a Twitter-style activity timeline, but filtered and actionable for race operations.

  • Incidents created or updated — with type, location, and current status
  • Key status changes: dispatched, resolved, escalated
  • Critical communications and system events
  • Staff movements and assignment changes
ⓘ  Design phase starting — mockups in progress.
